headline: How Nvidia Became a Chip Stock Loser—and Why It Might Stay That Way
summary: Nvidia  stock was edging up early Tuesday at the end of an underwhelming first half of the year.  The chip maker is looking to its next-generation hardware to spark a rally but gains might be muted for a while yet.  The major issue for Nvidia is that the huge spending on AI chips is now being spread out among a wider range of semiconductor companies.

headline: Chip stocks hit record 19.7% of S&P 500, quadrupling since 2020
summary: Nvidia sits at the center of this story as the single largest driver of the sector’s index weight expansion over the past few years amid the AI boom. However, the broadening of the semiconductor trade to include Broadcom, TSCM, ASML, AMD, and more recently memory stocks like Micron and SanDisk has pushed the index higher and higher.
